q.en: What is a New Year's resolution, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，什么是新年决心？

options.en: A promise to end a bad habit or begin a good one
options.zh: 一个结束坏习惯或开始好习惯的承诺

explain.en: The book says, 'A resolution is a promise to end a bad habit or begin a good one.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“决心是一个承诺，用来结束坏习惯或开始好习惯。”

q.en: In the United States, what famous event happens in Times Square on New Year's Eve?
q.zh: 在美国，时代广场除夕夜会发生什么著名事件？

options.en: A giant ball covered with lights drops from a tall building at midnight
options.zh: 午夜时，一个布满灯光的巨大球从高楼上降落

explain.en: In Times Square, 'a giant ball covered with lights drops from a tall building... At the stroke of midnight, the crowds cheer.'
explain.zh: 在时代广场，“一个布满灯光的巨大球从高楼上降落……午夜钟声敲响时，人群欢呼起来。”

q.en: In Central and South America, what do people do with twelve grapes?
q.zh: 在中美洲和南美洲，人们怎样对待十二颗葡萄？

options.en: Eat one grape in each of the last twelve seconds for good luck in each month
options.zh: 在最后十二秒里每秒吃一颗葡萄，为每个月祈求好运

explain.en: The book explains, 'Eating twelve grapes during the last twelve seconds of the year is a tradition... Each grape stands for one month of the coming year, and eating them all invites good luck.'
explain.zh: 书中解释：“在一年最后十二秒里吃十二颗葡萄是一个传统……每颗葡萄代表来年的一月，全部吃完会带来好运。”

q.en: In Peru, Panama, and Ecuador, what are muñecos, and what happens to them at midnight?
q.zh: 在秘鲁、巴拿马和厄瓜多尔，muñecos是什么，午夜时会发生什么？

options.en: Figures made of straw, rags, and paper that are paraded and then burned to release bad memories
options.zh: 用稻草、破布和纸做成的人偶，被游行展示后在午夜烧掉，以释放坏记忆
里面包着硬币的特别蛋糕

explain.en: In Peru, Panama, and Ecuador, people 'make muñecos... out of straw, rags, and paper... At midnight... set their muñecos on fire. Burning the figures represents the release of bad memories.'
explain.zh: 在秘鲁、巴拿马和厄瓜多尔，人们“用稻草、破布和纸制作muñecos（人偶）……午夜时……把人偶点燃烧掉。烧掉人偶代表释放过去的坏记忆。”

q.en: The month January is named for Janus, a Roman god shown with two faces. What does this represent?
q.zh: 一月是以Janus命名的，这位罗马神被画成两张脸。这代表了什么？

options.en: One face looks back at the old year, the other forward to the New Year
options.zh: 一张脸回望旧年，另一张脸展望新的一年

explain.en: The book says Janus 'is usually shown with two faces. One face looks backward at the old year and the other looks forward into the New Year.'
explain.zh: 书中说Janus“通常被画成两张脸。一张脸回望旧年，另一张脸展望新的一年。”

q.zh: 在苏格兰的Hogmanay，什么是“first-footing（第一个踏进门）”？

options.en: The year's first visitor should be a male with dark hair bearing gifts of coal, bread, and something to drink
options.zh: 一年里第一家访客必须是一位深色头发、带着煤、面包和饮料作礼物的男性

options.en: The first person to eat haggis
The first footrace of the year

explain.en: For Hogmanay first-footing, 'the year's first visitor to a home must be a male with dark hair bearing gifts of coal, bread, and something to drink.'
explain.zh: 在Hogmanay的“第一个踏进门”习俗中，“一年里第一家访客必须是一位深色头发、带着煤、面包和饮料作礼物的男性。”

q.en: How is the New Year celebrated in Thailand (Songkran)?

options.en: With a water festival (April 13-15) of parades spraying water and children with water blasters, because it is hot and dry
options.zh: 举行水节（4月13-15日），游行花车喷水、孩子们拿水枪互喷，因为那时又热又干

explain.en: In Thailand, 'the water festival is called Songkran... April 13 to 15. Parade floats spray crowds with water, and children chase one another with water blasters... a water fight is the perfect way to celebrate!'
explain.zh: 在泰国，“水节叫Songkran……4月13至15日。游行花车向人群喷水，孩子们拿着水枪互相追逐……水仗是完美的庆祝方式！”

q.en: Which New Year follows a lunar calendar that is about eleven days shorter than the 365-day solar year?
q.zh: 哪个新年遵循的阴历比365天的阳历年大约短十一天？

explain.en: The book states the Islamic New Year, Al-Hijra, 'is based on a lunar calendar... A lunar year is around eleven days shorter than the 365-day solar year.'
explain.zh: 书中说伊斯兰新年Al-Hijra“基于阴历……阴历年比365天的阳历年大约短十一天。”

options.en: To represent an idea with an object, picture, or sign
options.zh: 用一个物体、图画或符号来代表某种想法

explain.en: The glossary defines symbolize as 'to represent an idea with an object, picture, or sign.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将symbolize定义为“用一个物体、图画或符号来代表某种想法”。

q.en: What main idea does the book's conclusion give about New Year traditions worldwide?
q.zh: 书的结论关于全世界的新年传统，给出了什么主要观点？

options.en: Many traditions are shared internationally (cleaning, fireworks, resolutions) and show a hope for a fresh start
options.zh: 许多传统是国际共通的（大扫除、午夜烟花、立下决心），并怀着对新开始的希望

options.en: Only one correct way to celebrate exists
options.zh: 只有一种正确的庆祝方式

options.en: Fireworks were invented in the U.S.

explain.en: The conclusion says many traditions are 'shared internationally'—cleaning, fireworks, resolutions—and 'share a hope for a fresh start.'
explain.zh: 结论说许多传统是“国际共通的”——大扫除、午夜烟花、立下决心——并“怀着对新开始的希望”。

prompt.en: Choose one New Year tradition from the book and compare it to how your own family celebrates. What is similar or different?
prompt.zh: 从书中选一种新年传统，并与你自己的家庭庆祝方式比较。有什么相同或不同？

en: Name a specific tradition from the book (e.g., twelve grapes, Songkran water fight, or first-footing).
Tell one way your family's celebration is similar to or different from it.
zh: 说出书中一个具体传统（例如十二颗葡萄、Songkran水仗或第一个踏进门）。
说明你家庆祝方式与它的相同或不同之处。
